notes: Verified via WebSearch/WebFetch 2026-07-18: Royal Armed Forces founded 14 May 1956 by King Mohammed V, immediately after Moroccan independence.

King Mohammed VI, as Supreme Commander and Chief of the General Staff (القائد الأعلى ورئيس أركان الحرب العامة), issues an annual الأمر اليومي (Order of the Day) — the central ritual of the commemoration (confirmed via maroc.ma 69th-anniversary bulletin + Hespress).

Honest framing: this is a national military commemoration marked officially in barracks/units, NOT a paid public holiday in Morocco — distinguished in-content from عيد العرش (30 July) and عيد الاستقلال (18 Nov).
Local Darija month name '14 ماي' used (not '14 مايو'), matching green-march-morocco (6 نونبر) and throne-day-morocco (30 يوليوز).
Single-country event (_countryCode: ma), aliasSlugs: [] to avoid base_country_leakage; no hardcoded current/last-year digits — 1956 is a permitted historical year.
